_rationale: Iter-13 (DART-zdP14GNGfeo3): verify the compact `f`/`s` ref-key contract for context.save is honoured by both binaries. Direct probe in iter-13 confirmed Go REJECTS verbose `{file, symbol}` ref keys (`internal/types/context_manifest_types.go` ContextRef struct has `json:"f"` / `json:"s,omitempty"` — there is no `file`/`symbol` alias). C++ `manifest_from_json` (src/mcp/handlers_context.cpp) also reads only `f`/`s`. The compact-key contract is therefore mutual — this descriptor locks it. Iter-14 (DART-2PPeRKfyrceR) follow-up: the *output* manifest body was made Go-shape compliant in src/mcp/handlers_context.cpp manifest_to_json (top-level t/c/v/p/r/s, ref note `n`, LineRange `{s,e}`, stats block). The structural body parity is now verified by C++ unit test tests/integration/real_project_context_test.cpp::SaveAndLoadCompactKeysFileRoundTrip which asserts Go-shape keys on the on-disk manifest. The wire-byte parity diff cannot lift text to stable because the body still contains intrinsically per-call fields (`c` RFC3339Nano timestamp, `p` absolute project_root path) that require parser-aware diff to mask — promoting text to stable here would surface every wall-clock tick as a failure. See _rationale_ignore for the masking gap and the iter-14 unit-test bridge.

tiers._rationale_stable: Envelope frame: content[].type='text' and isError must match. A successful save on both sides means both accepted the compact `{f, s}` ref shape — the contract under test. result.isError absent or false on both → both binaries accepted; both true → both rejected (still parity, surface for triage). The textual manifest body itself stays ignore-tier; structural body parity is now bridged by the C++ unit test SaveAndLoadCompactKeysFileRoundTrip (iter-14).
tiers._rationale_ignore: Iter-14 (DART-2PPeRKfyrceR) fixed the body shape divergence at the emitter (manifest_to_json now emits Go-shape t/c/v/p/r/s with ref note `n` and LineRange `{s,e}`). The body cannot be promoted to stable on the wire-byte diff because: (a) `c` is an RFC3339Nano wall-clock timestamp per save call; (b) `p` is an absolute project_root path that resolves differently per binary's working directory; (c) the parity runner's spec_diff treats content[].text as an opaque leaf string (tests/lib/spec_diff/src/diff.cpp:62 — full string equality), so it cannot mask sub-string fields. Lifting text to stable would surface every save as a clock-tick failure. The structural assertion was moved to the C++ unit test tests/integration/real_project_context_test.cpp::SaveAndLoadCompactKeysFileRoundTrip which parses the on-disk manifest and verifies Go-shape keys (`t`/`c`/`v`/`r`/`s` present; `task`/`refs` absent; ref `f`/`s` correct). Karpathy rule 6 (no silent fallback): the gap is surfaced — a future parser-aware diff (e.g. content[].text.parse.<inner-path>) could lift text to stable; tracked as iter-14 follow-up loop-fix.
